title: fraxinus anomala subsp. lowellii
intro: Fraxinus anomala subsp. lowellii, also known as Pacific manzanita or Lowell's manzanita, is a subspecies of the Fraxinus anomala tree native to the Pacific coast of North America. The medicinal properties of Fraxinus anomala subsp. lowellii lie in its bark, which contains a compound called fraxin, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, acting as an antibacterial agent, and possessing antioxidant properties. Fraxinus anomala subsp. lowellii is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
